title: TUI runtime manifest at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/opencode/tui/<pid>.json (P4)
description:
Per docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-design.md 'Out of scope': add a TUI-side patch (in opencode-patched fork) that writes {pid, mode, cwd, url, sessionID, lastSeen} per TUI to $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/opencode/tui/<pid>.json. Heartbeat every few seconds, unlink on clean exit.

Solves the 'two bare TUIs in same cwd' ambiguity that P2.5 (workstation-2rn) collapses to a single restoration. Defer until the ambiguity proves problematic in practice.

Per ChatGPT's analysis (see /tmp/research-opencode-tui-restore-answer.md if still around): this is the cleanest way to make live TUI session id discoverable from outside the TUI process without DB schema changes, a new daemon, or forcing all bare invocations through serve.

Design context: docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-design.md

title: oc-auto-attach fails to restore opencode TUI after reset-workspace; manual ':te opencode' required
description:
After successful reset-workspace runs (verified by nightly-restart-background.service on 2026-04-27 03:00:06), step 6.5 (oc-auto-attach restoration of opencode TUIs) does NOT actually restore the TUI in any pane. User observation: pane %1 had nvim respawned (step 6 worked), but no opencode TUI appeared via :te. User had to manually run :te opencode to recover.

Most likely root cause:

opencode-serve restart at step 5 wipes in-memory session state. The session IDs captured in step 2 (BEFORE restart) are then no longer valid at GET /session/<id> after the restart. oc-auto-attach (in pkgs/oc-auto-attach/default.nix) waits up to 5s polling that endpoint for a non-empty .directory, then exits 0 silently if the wait times out. By design, oc-auto-attach exits 0 even on internal failure (so it doesn't break the launcher).

Code reference (oc-auto-attach):
  Step 1: 'wait for session to be visible with a non-empty directory'
  if ! session_dir="$(timeout 5 bash -c '...curl ... GET /session/$sid ...')"; then
    log "could not resolve session directory after 5s timeout"
    exit 0
  fi

Code reference (reset-workspace step 6.5, lines ~303-323 in deployed binary):
  if ! /home/dev/.nix-profile/bin/oc-auto-attach "\$sid" 2>&1; then
    log "  WARNING: oc-auto-attach \$sid returned non-zero"
  fi

Investigation needed:
1. Verify the hypothesis: query GET /session/<id> immediately after restart — does it return 404 / empty .directory for sessions that existed pre-restart?
2. If yes, decide approach:
   (a) Re-validate session ID post-restart and skip oc-auto-attach if invalid (silent skip)
   (b) Fall back to plain ':te opencode' (no -s) if session ID is invalid (creates fresh TUI, user observation matches what they did manually)
   (c) Persist sessions across opencode-serve restarts (likely a much bigger change in opencode itself)
   (d) Have reset-workspace stash the session list to disk and have post-restart oc-auto-attach use a session-list-restore path
3. Add explicit logging (not silent exit 0) when session resolution times out, so journal at least shows WHY restoration failed.

Reproduction:
  reset-workspace --yes  (with at least one nvim+opencode TUI pane in manifest)
  # observe: pane gets nvim respawned but no opencode TUI inside

Files:
  pkgs/reset-workspace/default.nix (step 6.5 calling oc-auto-attach)
  pkgs/oc-auto-attach/default.nix (the silent-exit-0 timeout)
  /var/log/journal entries from 2026-04-27 03:00:06 (nightly run with the issue)

Discovered from: workstation-pqu (cgroup-survival fix that revealed this gap once the script could actually reach step 6.5).
notes:
2026-04-27 INVESTIGATION CORRECTION + DESIGN APPROVED:

Original hypothesis (opencode-serve restart wipes session memory) was WRONG.
opencode-serve persists sessions to ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db and
reloads them on restart; GET /session/<sid> works fine post-restart.

ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE (proven via journal evidence + log-file analysis):
At 03:00:06 nightly run, the only opencode TUI alive was a stuck/early-init
bare `:te opencode` (cmdline `/home/dev/.nix-profile/bin/opencode`, no args)
whose log file (040130.log, 1781 bytes) contained only plugin-loader
output and zero `path=/session/` lines. Step 2's argv match failed (no
sid in argv) and log-file fallback failed (no session log lines yet).
Result: OPENCODE_COUNT=0, oc-auto-attach was NEVER CALLED.

The bug is in reset-workspace step 2 session snapshot, NOT in oc-auto-attach.

DESIGN APPROVED (2026-04-27):
docs/plans/2026-04-27-reset-workspace-snapshot-fix-design.md

Approach B (minimal + observability):
- Replace step 2 with strict pgrep on `opencode attach.*--session ses_`
  pattern (covers /launch + opencode-launch sessions reliably).
- Drop log-file fallback entirely.
- Add observability WARNING per bare TUI being skipped.
- New contract: only /launch + opencode-launch sessions survive reset.
- Document in .opencode/skills/resetting-workspace/SKILL.md.

Rejected alternatives:
- Pigeon-cwd join: pigeon `state` field is unusable (always "running"),
  TTL-based liveness lags by minutes/hours. Would only matter for sessions
  with no live process — out of scope.
- Patching opencode-patched to surface sid: bigger change, deferred.

Next: writing-plans skill → implementation plan → ship.
close_reason: Fixed via strict pgrep on attach-client argv pattern. Step 2 now captures /launch + opencode-launch sessions reliably (one captured + observability WARNINGs for bare TUIs that won't survive). Verified live 2026-04-27 via nightly-restart-background.service: journal shows 'snapshotting live opencode attach clients...', 'WARNING: bare opencode TUI pid=4135065 ...', 'captured 0 restorable session(s); 1 bare TUI(s) skipped', no spurious false-positive WARNINGs (exe-basename guard works). Design: docs/plans/2026-04-27-reset-workspace-snapshot-fix-design.md. Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-27-reset-workspace-snapshot-fix-plan.md. Commits: 08780ce (code) + cd6eff2 (skill doc). Followups filed for P2.5 (bare-TUI restoration via cwd->sid), P4 (runtime manifest), P3 (OPENCODE_ATTACH_URL opt-in).

title: Restore bare opencode TUIs across reset via cwd→sid resolution (P2.5)
description:
Extends workstation-0gu's narrow strict-pgrep fix to also restore bare ':te opencode' TUIs across reset. For each bare TUI alive at snapshot time, resolve /proc/<pid>/cwd to the most-recent root session via opencode-serve's GET /session?directory=...&roots=true&limit=1 endpoint (verified empirically against the live serve), then restore as an attach client in step 6.5.

Net effect: every opencode TUI alive at snapshot time survives reset. Post-restoration, all restored TUIs are attach clients (uniform contract for next reset).

Design: docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-design.md
Plan:   docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-plan.md

Discovered-from: workstation-0gu

Implementation deferred to next session per user direction. The plan is fully specified with exact code, expected outputs, and a critical landmine (live verification MUST go via 'sudo systemctl --no-block start nightly-restart-background.service', NOT bash inline, to avoid SIGHUP killing the script when the orchestrator's TUI dies).
close_reason:
Implemented per design + plan and deployed via 'nix run home-manager -- switch --flake .#cloudbox'. Live-tested via 'sudo systemctl --no-block start nightly-restart-background.service' on 2026-04-28T09:04:50.

JOURNAL EVIDENCE: 'captured 3 restorable session(s) (raw: 3 strict-attach + 0 bare-resolved; dedupe may collapse); 0 bare TUI(s) skipped' — confirms the new code path executed cleanly with the new summary format and counter scheme. No crashes, no script aborts (the three || true defensive fixes from code review held). Strict-attach loop continues to work (workstation-0gu non-regression).

CAVEAT 1 (test gap): the bare-resolution branch was NOT live-exercised in production traffic because all 3 alive opencode TUIs at trigger time were already attach clients (orchestrator was attach pid 4157355 by then; the worktree TUIs for cul/int were also attach clients from prior /launch). Branch correctness verified statically only: spec-compliance review ✅, code-quality review ✅ after fixing 3 review findings (curl|jq pipefail, cmdline TOCTOU, broken strict-count math), nix build clean, grep checks pass.

CAVEAT 2 (premise undermined by separate bug): the design's premise — that uniform attach-client restoration is valuable because next reset captures everything via argv — depends on attach mode itself being functional. The post-reset live test surfaced workstation-gsi (P1, restored attach TUIs frozen — no SSE-driven UI updates), which means P2.5's net effect today is 'more sessions restored, but all of them frozen post-restore (Telegram-only interaction)'. Filed workstation-gsi for next session.

ALSO FILED: workstation-io7 (oc-auto-attach window-name shorthand mismatch causes duplicate windows), workstation-dfx (nvim-treesitter init error blocks oc-auto-attach socket-helper).

Commits: feat(reset-workspace) 15770f7, docs(skill) 4ffbbe3, plan b8dd8b2.

Reopen if (a) workstation-gsi resolves and we want a true live test of the bare-resolution branch, or (b) post-fix testing reveals the resolved bare branch has any production bug not caught by static review.

title: Opt-in OPENCODE_ATTACH_URL: route bare opencode through opencode-serve (P3)
description:
Per docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-design.md 'Out of scope': patch packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/thread.ts (in opencode-patched fork) to detect a healthy $OPENCODE_ATTACH_URL/global/health before Worker creation. If healthy, construct the TUI's transport with native HTTP fetch to that URL instead of spawning a private worker. Falls back to worker on failure. Opt-in via env var (NOT silent default).

Considerations:
- MCP/tool scoping (per-TUI worker vs long-lived serve)
- Reconnect-on-serve-restart behavior (today's bare TUIs lose connection if serve dies)
- Auth/password if serve config requires
- Why source patch (not wrapper script): inheritance for IDE/scripts/internal invocations
- Carefully preserve --session, --continue, --fork, --agent, prompt args

Per ChatGPT's analysis: NOT a tiny 'change the transport object' patch — server discovery must happen BEFORE Worker creation, with early return paths to avoid starting (or later stopping) the worker.

Reference upstream issues:
- sst/opencode#8948 (Server Registry & Auto-Discovery)
- sst/opencode#7629 (Connect to existing server instead of starting new)
- sst/opencode#6461 (Named Remote Connections)
- sst/opencode#17322 (Config option to auto-attach to remote server)

Pre-requisite for closing: confirm P2.5 (workstation-2rn) is shipped; review interactions with multi-cwd workspace handling.

Design context: docs/plans/2026-04-27-bare-tui-restoration-design.md

title: nvim init fails with 'nvim-treesitter.configs not found' — blocks oc-auto-attach socket-helper
description:
nvim init.lua fails to load with the following error during startup:

  Error detected while processing /home/dev/.config/nvim/init.lua:
  E5113: Error while calling lua chunk: /home/dev/.config/nvim/lua/user/telescope.lua:2: module 'nvim-treesitter.configs' not found:
          no field package.preload['nvim-treesitter.configs']
          no file '/nix/store/zk55gcqn8rjsinh9lrb10mv10xsvz4vi-luajit-2.1.1741730670-env/share/lua/5.1/nvim-treesitter/configs.lua'
          no file '/nix/store/zk55gcqn8rjsinh9lrb10mv10xsvz4vi-luajit-2.1.1741730670-env/share/lua/5.1/nvim-treesitter/configs/init.lua'
          ...
  stack traceback:
          [C]: in function 'require'
          /home/dev/.config/nvim/lua/user/telescope.lua:2: in main chunk
          [C]: in function 'require'
          /home/dev/.config/nvim/init.lua:6: in main chunk
  Press ENTER or type command to continue

OBSERVED: When oc-auto-attach creates a fresh tmux window/pane and spawns nvim there, nvim halts at the "Press ENTER" prompt due to this treesitter error. The user-helper autocmd that creates /tmp/nvim-N.sock never runs, so oc-auto-attach gives up after 5 seconds with "nvim at /tmp/nvim-N.sock not ready (or helper not loaded) after 5s; giving up". The session is captured in OPENCODE_MANIFEST but is not actually wired into a TUI pane.

Confirmed in P2.5 live test journal at 09:05:03 + 09:05:08 for both restored worktree sessions (ses_22c80b3..., ses_22bd29d74...).

ROOT CAUSE: lua/user/telescope.lua:2 calls `require("nvim-treesitter.configs")` but that module is not in the runtimepath / Lua package path. Either:
- The home-manager nvim package doesn't include nvim-treesitter (or includes it without exposing the configs module).
- The dotfiles-managed nvim init.lua has a stale require that should have been migrated to lazy-load or removed.
- The plugin manager (lazy.nvim?) is supposed to install it but the package isn't reaching the right path.

FIX DIRECTION: inspect /home/dev/.config/nvim/lua/user/telescope.lua and /home/dev/.config/nvim/init.lua to understand the plugin assumption; cross-reference with users/dev/home.cloudbox.nix or the dotfiles config to see whether nvim-treesitter is supposed to be provided. May also require running :Lazy sync or similar inside nvim to populate it.

USER IMPACT: blocks oc-auto-attach restoration for any project where the user does not have a pre-existing nvim with the treesitter package already loaded (e.g. fresh tmux window from reset). Compounds with workstation-gsi (frozen attach) and the window-name duplicate bug.

Discovered-from: workstation-2rn (P2.5 live test 2026-04-28).

title: reset-workspace fails to respawn nvims and restore opencode TUIs
close_reason: Duplicate of workstation-pqu (created ~2s apart, same title, no description). bd sync race during initial create — keeping workstation-pqu as the canonical bead.

title: Restored opencode attach TUIs are frozen — no UI updates despite open SSE; user must use Telegram
description:
Restored opencode attach TUIs render NO new updates after restoration: TUI display stays frozen at pre-restore state even while session is fully alive server-side.

USER IMPACT: "dead in the water" (verbatim). User can interact with restored sessions ONLY via the Telegram → pigeon → opencode-serve bridge; the in-tmux nvim TUI itself is unusable.

OBSERVED: P2.5 live test 2026-04-28T09:04:50, after nightly-restart-background.service ran and oc-auto-attach restored 3 sessions. Within minutes of restoration, all 3 attach TUIs (workstation/cul/int) showed frozen rendering. User's in-bound messages routed via Telegram (pigeon journal `[command-ingest] routing as question reply sessionId=ses_2352361... commandId=...` at 09:16:01) were processed correctly server-side and emitted via outbox to telegram, but the local TUI display showed neither inbound messages nor the orchestrator's outbound responses.

EVIDENCE:
- TCP connectivity healthy: orchestrator's attach client (pid 4181849) shows two ESTAB sockets to 127.0.0.1:4096 (fds 18 + 27, presumably one HTTP + one SSE), both with Recv-Q=0 Send-Q=0.
- /event SSE endpoint emits {"type":"server.connected","properties":{}} immediately on connect, then NOTHING during 8 seconds of active orchestrator tool-call activity. Same for /event?sessionID=ses_2352361...
- opencode-serve.service journal is silent post-startup: only emits "opencode server listening on http://127.0.0.1:4096" at 09:04:57, then ZERO further entries for 14+ minutes despite handling many session events.
- Pigeon receives outbox events fine (proves session events ARE happening server-side), so the missing piece is the SSE broadcast to attach clients.

NOT A P2.5 REGRESSION: workstation-0gu's strict-attach restoration uses the same oc-auto-attach mechanism, produces the same kind of attach-client TUIs. We never UI-tested those restorations (verified via journal only); the freeze most likely existed there too and we just didn't catch it.

LIKELY ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESES (in order of plausibility, untested):
1. opencode-serve doesn't broadcast session message-update events to /event subscribers when the session-update originates from a non-TUI source (pigeon API calls vs an attach POST). Need to read SSE broadcast code in ~/projects/opencode/packages/opencode/src/server/.
2. SSE subscription URL the attach client uses doesn't include a sessionID filter (or filters incorrectly), so events for OTHER sessions reach it but events for ITS session don't. Read subscribe logic in ~/projects/opencode/packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/attach.tsx.
3. opencode-patched fork has a regression in one of its 5 patches (eager-input-streaming, mcp-reconnect, prefill-fix, tool-fix, vim) that breaks SSE event push. Bisect against upstream.
4. Race condition where attach-client's SSE subscription is registered BEFORE opencode-serve's session pre-loaded, and never gets re-registered when the session becomes active.

DEBUGGING ENTRY POINTS:
- ~/projects/opencode/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/global.ts (SSE setup)
- ~/projects/opencode/packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/session.ts (per-session events)
- ~/projects/opencode/packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/attach.tsx (attach-client subscribe logic)
- ~/projects/opencode-patched/patches/ (5 patches; one may be the culprit)

SUGGESTED FIRST EXPERIMENT: spawn a fresh `opencode attach http://127.0.0.1:4096 --session ses_xxx` for an EXISTING active session in a new pane, immediately type something via the new TUI, observe whether the new TUI is also frozen. If yes → attach mode generally broken. If no → restoration via oc-auto-attach has a specific bug.

WORKAROUND (current): use Telegram bot to interact with frozen attach sessions.

BLOCKER STATUS: P2.5 (workstation-2rn) shipped but its premise — that uniform attach-client restoration is valuable — depends on this bug being fixed. Until it is, P2.5's net effect is "more sessions restored, but all of them frozen post-restore". Telegram side-channel preserves communication; in-TUI experience is broken.

Discovered-from: workstation-2rn (P2.5 live test 2026-04-28).

title: Investigate reset-workspace step 5 silent no-op: sudo systemctl restart returns 0 but opencode-serve does not restart
description:
During the live verification of workstation-pqu (cgroup-survival fix) on 2026-04-27 00:01:08, the always-detached reset-workspace --yes script reached step 5 ('restarting opencode-serve.service...') and invoked /run/wrappers/bin/sudo systemctl restart opencode-serve.service. Journal evidence:

  Apr 27 00:01:08 sudo[2685441]: pam_unix(sudo:session): session opened for user root(uid=0) by dev(uid=1000)
  Apr 27 00:01:08 sudo[2685441]: pam_unix(sudo:session): session closed for user root

The sudo session opened AND closed cleanly (suggests the wrapped systemctl returned 0). However:

- No `Stopping OpenCode headless serve` / `Stopped` / `Started` events appeared in the journal for opencode-serve.service.
- opencode-serve PID remained 2653689 from 14:16:48 (~10h prior) — never restarted.
- The script's tee'd stdout log stops at 'restarting opencode-serve.service...' with no subsequent line — neither the success path's 'polling /global/health for serve readiness...' nor the failure path's 'failed to restart opencode-serve' (which `die` would emit).

Hypotheses to investigate:
1. The detached scope's stdout was severed (SIGPIPE through tee?) immediately after the script printed 'restarting...', killing the script mid-sudo. But sudo session still closed cleanly — would the script have to survive past sudo for that to happen?
2. systemctl restart silently no-op'd due to a race condition (concurrent transaction, unit state, etc).
3. The pipeline `reset-workspace --yes 2>&1 | tee /tmp/log` is incompatible with always-detach-via-systemd-run because --scope inherits stdout but the parent pipe (tee) may close prematurely once systemd-run exec returns... but `systemd-run --scope` blocks until the scope exits, so this shouldn't apply.
4. Bash tool reported 'User aborted' but user did NOT abort. The reported abort is a misnomer for some other backend disconnection.

Reproduction:
  cd ~/projects/workstation
  reset-workspace --yes 2>&1 | tee /tmp/repro.log
  # Watch journal: sudo journalctl -u opencode-serve.service --since "1 min ago" --no-pager

Files:
- pkgs/reset-workspace/default.nix (step 5 at line ~263-273 in the always-detach version)
- /tmp/reset-workspace-task5-live.log (output of 2026-04-27 00:01 run)
- Journal: Apr 27 00:01:08 entries

This bug does NOT affect the cgroup-survival fix (workstation-pqu, closed). The script reaches step 5 — that's already an improvement from previous failures where it died before. But the restart silently failing means the script's primary purpose (refresh opencode-serve) doesn't fully work.
close_reason: INVALID: subsequent investigation revealed the cgroup-survival fix actually works end-to-end. Evidence: nightly-restart-background.service ran reset-workspace at 2026-04-27 03:00:06, the script logged 'detaching into fresh user systemd scope...', restarted opencode-serve cleanly (PID 2692107 from 03:00:07 timestamp), and the systemd unit exited 0/SUCCESS. The original 00:01:08 'silent no-op' observation that prompted this bead was likely an artifact of the live test invocation (bash-tool + tee-pipe + always-detach interaction), NOT a real bug in reset-workspace itself. The actual remaining issue is in step 6.5 (oc-auto-attach) — filed as new bead.

title: oc-auto-attach creates duplicate tmux windows when user has shortcut-named existing windows
description:
oc-auto-attach derives the tmux window name from the session.directory's BASENAME (e.g. /home/dev/projects/culinary-operations-server → "culinary-operations-server"), and only matches existing windows by exact name. Users who manually shorten window names (e.g. "cul" for "culinary-operations-server", "int" for "internal-frontends") get DUPLICATE windows on every restoration: the original "cul" window stays, and a NEW "culinary-operations-server" window is created alongside it.

OBSERVED: P2.5 live test 2026-04-28T09:04:50. User had pre-existing tmux windows "cul" (cwd /home/dev/projects/culinary-operations-server) and "int" (cwd /home/dev/projects/internal-frontends). After reset, oc-auto-attach restored ses_22c80b3... (cul-server worktree) and ses_22bd29d74... (int-frontends worktree), creating NEW windows "culinary-operations-server" and "internal-frontends" instead of reusing the existing short-named ones.

JOURNAL EVIDENCE (Apr 28 09:04:58):
  [oc-auto-attach] session ses_22c80b3... dir=/home/dev/projects/culinary-operations-server/.worktrees/pr-4202
  [oc-auto-attach] project_key=/home/dev/projects/culinary-operations-server window_name=culinary-operations-server
  [oc-auto-attach] created new pane %70 (window culinary-operations-server)

SAME PATTERN for ses_22bd29d74... → window "internal-frontends" (duplicate of user's "int").

UX IMPACT: stale window state accretes over time. User had to manually inspect 5 tmux windows post-reset to figure out which had usable nvims. Compounds with workstation-gsi (frozen attach) because the new windows have failed nvim init (treesitter error) → user has to know which window is "the real one".

POTENTIAL FIXES (rough sketch):
1. Allow per-project window-name aliases via a config file (e.g. ~/.config/oc-auto-attach/window-aliases.json mapping cwd → tmux window-name).
2. Match existing windows by checking pane current_path against session.directory (semantic matching, not name-based).
3. Add a tmux user variable @oc_project_key on windows to opt them into oc-auto-attach restoration, then match on that.

NOT URGENT: cosmetic/UX issue; user can manually rename or kill duplicate windows. Lower priority than the frozen-attach bug.

Discovered-from: workstation-2rn (P2.5 live test 2026-04-28).

title: Prune stale opencode sessions from DB
description:
**Context:** As of the 2026-04-25 reset-workspace TUI restoration work (see commits 8ee5231..b5a6751), reset-workspace no longer DELETEs opencode sessions on each reset — they must persist so oc-auto-attach can re-bind TUIs to them after the kill+respawn cycle. Without a sibling cleanup job, the DB grows monotonically.

**Current state:**
- DB location: `~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db` (SQLite).
- Schema: `session` table.
- Count check: `nix run nixpkgs#sqlite -- ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db 'SELECT count(*) FROM session'`
- Approximate size as of plan write: ~1500 sessions. Growth is slow but unbounded.

**Goal:** Build a cleanup mechanism that prunes stale sessions WITHOUT breaking active ones.

**Open design questions:**
- What defines 'stale'? Last-message age? No `opencode attach` invocation in N days? No mention in any opencode log file? Prefer a conservative definition (e.g. sessions older than 30 days with no activity in the last 14).
- Where should it run? Standalone systemd unit/timer (simplest, isolated)? Bolted onto nightly-restart-background.service before snapshot (atomic with the reset cycle)? Separate cron?
- Direct SQLite delete vs HTTP API DELETE? API used to work (the deleted reset-workspace block called `curl -sf -X DELETE$OPENCODE_URL/session/$id`); SQLite is simpler but bypasses any in-memory caches opencode-serve may hold. Verify behavior.
- How to identify 'in-use' sessions to NEVER delete? Cross-reference with `pgrep -u dev -f opencode` cmdlines (the same logic reset-workspace already has) before any DELETE.

**Files to modify:**
- New: `pkgs/opencode-session-prune/default.nix` (or similar) — the cleanup tool.
- New: `hosts/cloudbox/configuration.nix` — systemd unit/timer entry.
- Possibly update: `.opencode/skills/resetting-workspace/SKILL.md` to point at the new tool.

**Reference:**
- Original reset-workspace design: `docs/plans/2026-04-24-reset-workspace-design.md`
- TUI restoration design: `docs/plans/2026-04-25-reset-workspace-restore-design.md` (see 'DB growth' note)
- TUI restoration plan risk register: `docs/plans/2026-04-25-reset-workspace-restore-plan.md` (last entry)
- Companion `reap-stale-opencode` cron: `hosts/cloudbox/configuration.nix:428-491` — uses similar pgrep-based liveness logic, useful pattern reference.

**Acceptance:**
- Session count stays bounded (e.g. < 500 most of the time).
- No live session ever deleted (verified by spot-checks before any DELETE).
- Cleanup runs autonomously (timer-driven), with logs visible in journalctl.
- `reset-workspace` continues to work end-to-end after the cleanup job is in place.

title: reset-workspace fails to respawn nvims and restore opencode TUIs
description:
Observed during v1.14.25 rollout on cloudbox at 2026-04-26 ~14:14 EDT:

Ran 'reset-workspace --yes' to pick up new opencode-patched-1.14.25 binary
across the daemon and all TUIs. The command appears to have:

1. SIGKILLed all nvims (verified: only window 0's nvim alive afterwards;
   bash panes in windows 1-3 are now bare prompts).
2. NOT restarted opencode-serve.service to the new binary (still on
   /nix/store/5cjn6rh8...-1.14.20, not the new 1.14.25 derivation
   /nix/store/pm6yjbvl...-1.14.25 we just installed).
3. NOT respawned nvims via nvims in the original cwds.
4. NOT restored opencode TUIs via oc-auto-attach.

Then user reported the command 'didn't open nvims sessions or opencode
sessions' and aborted/observed it.

The symptom suggests reset-workspace either silently failed early (e.g.
during the snapshot phase before SIGKILL, or during the systemctl
restart step) and left the system half-torn-down, OR the respawn loop
hit an error after SIGKILL.

Need to investigate:
- /tmp/reset-workspace.lock state when this ran
- reset-workspace exit code
- Whether opencode-serve restart even attempted (sudo systemctl
  list-jobs / journalctl -u opencode-serve.service since 14:14)
- The reset-workspace script source (likely under
  ~/projects/workstation/users/dev or pkgs/) for what step might have
  silently swallowed an error

Workaround for the immediate situation: manually respawn nvims via
nvims in each detached pane, manually 'sudo systemctl restart
opencode-serve.service' to pick up the new 1.14.25 binary.
notes:
UPDATE 2026-04-26 (post-implementation, pre-final-test):

The original cgroup-only fix was insufficient. Two failure modes were identified:

MODE A (cgroup membership): script invoked from agent attached to opencode-serve.service is in that service's cgroup; sudo systemctl restart kills the whole cgroup including the script.

MODE B (PTY collapse / process-tree): script invoked from agent's bash tool inside an nvims term:// inside tmux is a great-great-grandchild of nvim. Step 4 (pkill -9 -u dev -x nvim) kills the ancestor nvim → PTY collapses → SIGHUP propagates down to the script (no trap) → script dies before step 5 (restart serve).

FIX SHIPPED in commits 2d78f04 (code) + 99b83ef (doc), local only — NOT YET PUSHED. The fix always re-execs into a `systemd-run --user --scope --collect --quiet -- "$0" "$@"` transient unit at script entry. The new scope:
- Has its own cgroup (defeats Mode A)
- Has its own session leader / no controlling TTY (defeats Mode B)
- Is reparented under user@1000.service (no nvim ancestor)

Gated by RESET_WORKSPACE_DETACHED loop guard + RESET_WORKSPACE_NO_DETACH escape hatch.

CRITICAL DISCOVERIES en route:
1. systemd-run `--pty` is rejected in --scope mode (it's a service-only flag). Initial fix had --pty; was discovered + removed.
2. The detach block MUST live BEFORE the arg-parser while-loop. Otherwise `shift` consumes --yes and the re-exec'd instance prompts and dies. First attempt placed it AFTER the parser; controlled test caught it.

VERIFICATION STATUS:
- Task 3 synthetic repro of Mode A: PASSED (process forced into opencode-serve cgroup re-execs into run-pXXX.scope outside it)
- Empirical test of Mode B: PASSED twice (script reached step 4 region without dying from SIGHUP, even though my agent's parent chain has nvim ancestor)
- Live end-to-end with --yes: NOT YET PASSED (binary on PATH is still the broken a5e902e version that loses --yes; needs `nix run home-manager -- switch --flake .#cloudbox` first)

LANDMINE: subagents (implementer agent type) cannot be trusted with explicit "DO NOT RUN THE LIVE INVOCATION" instructions. One implementer ignored that instruction and ran reset-workspace --yes anyway, destroying the user's nvim session. For destructive runtime invocations, the orchestrator must run them inline.

NEXT STEPS:
1. git push the two unpushed commits
2. nix run home-manager -- switch --flake .#cloudbox
3. Verify on-PATH binary has the move (grep for 'Process detachment' before 'Parse args' in the rendered script)
4. Safe smoke test: RESET_WORKSPACE_DETACHED=1 reset-workspace --yes (should hit "(--yes: skipping confirmation)" line, no [y/N] prompt)
5. Live test ONLY after explicit user go-ahead; orchestrator runs it inline, NOT delegated
6. Close bead, sync, push, verify clean
close_reason: Fixed: reset-workspace now always re-execs into a fresh systemd-run --user --scope at script entry (before arg parsing), giving it (a) its own cgroup outside opencode-serve.service (defeats KillMode=control-group), (b) its own session leader / no controlling TTY (defeats SIGHUP from killed ancestor nvim), and (c) reparented under user@1000.service (no nvim ancestor in chain). Verified empirically by live invocation 2026-04-27 00:01: script logged 'detaching into fresh user systemd scope', survived past pkill -9 -u dev -x nvim (proves Mode B fix), reached step 5 (sudo systemctl restart opencode-serve.service was logged in journal). A separate, unrelated bug was discovered at step 5 where sudo returns 0 but systemd does not actually restart opencode-serve — filed as follow-up.
